# @nifrajs/web-vanilla — LLM contract card

<!-- GENERATED by scripts/gen-llm-cards.ts. Do not edit by hand — run `bun run gen:cards`. -->

Zero-framework render adapter for @nifrajs/web — auto-escaping tagged-template HTML, ~0 KB client JS, interactivity via @nifrajs/web/islands.

> A tight contract card for AI agents: the exports you actually call + the footguns. For the full
> reference see [`api-reference.md`](../../api-reference.md) (every export + signature) and
> [`llms-full.txt`](../../llms-full.txt) (the prose guides). One cheap read instead of the whole corpus.

## Key exports

- **compose** _(function)_ — `compose: (chain: readonly unknown[], props: RenderProps) => Template`
- **html** _(function)_ — `html: (strings: TemplateStringsArray, ...values: HtmlValue[]) => Template`
- **raw** _(function)_ — `raw: (trusted: string) => RawHtml`
- **RawHtml** _(class)_ — `class RawHtml`
- **Template** _(class)_ — `class Template`
- **vanillaAdapter** _(const)_ — `vanillaAdapter: RenderAdapter`
- **HtmlValue** _(type)_ — `type HtmlValue = | string | number | bigint | boolean | null | undefined | Template | RawHtml | ReadonlyArray<HtmlValue>`
- **VanillaComponent** _(type)_ — `type VanillaComponent = (props: RenderProps & { children?: Template }) => Template`

## Footguns

- Output is **auto-escaping** tagged-template HTML — interpolated values are escaped; opt into raw HTML explicitly, and only for trusted content.
- Interactivity comes from islands (`@nifrajs/web/islands` + `@nifrajs/islets`), not a framework runtime — ~0 KB client JS by default.
